No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to eradicating poverty or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to ending hunger or improving food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to health, well-being, or related targets.

Quality Education.reason: The course provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops technical, vocational, and entrepreneurial skills (Target 4.4) through hands-on project development and system capabilities enhancement.
evidence: Profession of information technology and knowledge of business innovation
enhance their capabilities in system planning, analysis, design, development, launch, and maintenance

Gender Equality.reason: The course's focus on information and communications technology (ICT) development can be inferred as an 'enabling technology' to promote women's empowerment (Target 5.b), though the connection is indirect.
evidence: develop a working system for a real-life application

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to energy access or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly promotes 'business innovation' and '訂定商業模式' (define business model), fostering entrepreneurship and productive activities (Target 8.3) and enhancing economic productivity (Target 8.2).
evidence: 訂定商業模式 (define business model)
develop a working system for a real-life application

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's core focus is on 'Information Systems Project,' involving 'computer programming,' 'information technologies,' 'machine learning toolkits,' and 'IT innovation,' directly aligning with Targets 9.5 (innovation) and 9.c (ICT access).
evidence: attend other outside contests related to IT innovation

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to reducing income or social inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to urban development or sustainable communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The 'Social responsibility and ethics' core competency can be weakly inferred to encourage sustainable business practices (Target 12.6) in system development and operation.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to climate change mitigation or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The explicit instruction to 'Respect Copyright' directly supports the rule of law (Target 16.3), and 'Social responsibility and ethics' broadly contributes to strong institutions.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course emphasizes 'Students work in teams' and 'Teamwork and leadership abilities,' and mentions '招攬上下游廠商、使用者登錄上線' (recruit vendors/users), directly supporting multi-stakeholder partnerships (Target 17.17) and ICT use (Target 17.8).
evidence: 招攬上下游廠商、使用者登錄上線 (recruit upstream/downstream vendors and users)
